title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
summary: Studio is an internal workspace that lets users ask natural language questions against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion. The LLM generates reusable declarative JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, making subsequent runs deterministic and cheap. Reliability features include preflight sequencing to manage context windows, layering rules to prioritize primary sources, and query validation before widget hardcoding.
writeup: Garrett Galow of WorkOS presents Studio, an internal workspace that lets non-technical users ask business questions in natural language against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion without writing SQL. Rather than re-running an LLM for every query, the system generates reusable declarative JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, so subsequent runs become deterministic and cost-effective. The talk covers the practical mechanics behind this approach, including context window optimization, preflight sequencing, and query validation.
